info.title: Laminus — OrcaSlicer Sidecar API
info.description:
Headless 3D model slicing via OrcaSlicer CLI running inside a Docker container. Laminus is a sidecar service used by Themis; it is not intended to be called directly by end-users in normal operation.

## Typical agent workflow

1. **`GET /api/health`** — wait until `catalog_loaded: true` before making any other calls.
2. **`GET /api/profiles`** — discover machine / process / filament UUIDs for your printer and material. Filter by `manufacturer`, `model`, and `nozzle` to narrow results.
3. **`POST /api/slice/start`** — upload the model (`.3mf` or `.stl`) with the resolved UUIDs. The response contains a `job_id`.
4. **`GET /api/slice/status/{job_id}`** — poll every 2–5 s until `status` is `completed` or `failed`.
5. **`GET /api/slice/download/{job_id}`** — retrieve the GCode (or `.3mf` when `export_3mf` was requested). Downloading evicts the job immediately.

Alternatively, use **`POST /api/slice/prepared`** when the 3MF already embeds print settings from a previous OrcaSlicer session — no profile resolution is needed.

## Packing / arrangement

Use **`POST /api/pack`** to distribute STL geometry across build plates before slicing. Three modes: supply a template `.3mf`, profile UUIDs (preferred — catalog-aware), or explicit bed dimensions. The packed `.3mf` is returned directly (synchronous, up to 120 s).

## Error conventions

- **422** — caller-side validation failure (bad UUIDs, missing required fields, etc.).
- **503** — Laminus is not yet ready; the catalog is still building. Retry after `GET /api/health` reports `catalog_loaded: true`.
- **400 / 408 / 500** — OrcaSlicer-side failures; check server logs for details.

get.description:
Returns all machine, process, and filament presets known to this container.

**Filtering:** supply all three of `manufacturer`, `model`, and `nozzle` together to receive only the matching machine preset plus all process and filament presets that declare that machine in their `compatible_printers` list. Supplying only one or two of the three parameters is a **422** error.

**Refresh:** pass `refresh=true` to schedule a background catalog rebuild (e.g., after uploading new profiles via `POST /api/profiles/upload`). The response reflects the *current* catalog immediately; the new catalog is available after ~5 s. While rebuilding, `GET /api/health` will show `catalog_building: true`.

**Response shape:**
```json
{
  "machine":  [{ "uuid": "...", "name": "...", "manufacturer": "...", "model": "...", "nozzle": "..." }],
  "process":  [{ "uuid": "...", "name": "...", "layer_height": 0.2, "compatible_printers": [...] }],
  "filament": [{ "uuid": "...", "name": "...", "filament_type": "PLA", "filament_colour": "#FFFFFF", ... }]
}
```

Returns **503** while the catalog is initialising on container start.

422.description: Partial filter: supply all three of manufacturer/model/nozzle or none

503.description: Catalog not yet ready — retry after /api/health reports catalog_loaded: true

post.summary: Get merged project_settings.config for a profile set
post.description:
Resolves the full inheritance chains for the given machine, process, and filament UUIDs and returns the merged `project_settings.config` dict — exactly what would be embedded inside a `.3mf` before slicing.

**Primary use cases:**
- Inspect resolved print settings (temperatures, speeds, gcode) before committing to a slice job.
- Allow Themis to diff embedded settings against a chosen preset to warn the user of overrides (`POST /api/v1/jobs/check-overrides`).
- Verify that `layer_gcode` is populated (see OrcaSlicer -51 error) before slicing.

The response is the raw merged dict with inheritance stripped — keys like `inherits`, `compatible_printers`, `is_custom_defined`, and `from` are removed. Multi-filament keys are returned as arrays (one value per slot).

Returns **503** while the catalog is initialising.

422.description: UUID not found in catalog, wrong type (e.g. process UUID used where machine expected), or filament_uuids is empty

503.description: Catalog not yet ready — retry after /api/health reports catalog_loaded: true

get.summary: Get full detail for one profile
get.description:
Returns all public fields for a single profile entry identified by its stable UUID. The `_resolved` (inheritance-flattened) dict is not included — use `POST /api/profiles/merged-config` for the fully merged config.

**Fields by type:**

*Machine:* `uuid`, `name`, `manufacturer`, `model`, `nozzle`, `nozzle_diameter`, `bed_size_x`, `bed_size_y`, `extruder_count`, `source`, `rel_path`

*Process:* `uuid`, `name`, `layer_height`, `compatible_printers`, `source`, `rel_path`

*Filament:* `uuid`, `name`, `display_name`, `filament_type`, `filament_colour`, `filament_vendor`, `filament_diameter`, `filament_density`, `nozzle_temperature`, `nozzle_temperature_range_low`, `nozzle_temperature_range_high`, `bed_temperature`, `bed_temperature_initial_layer`, `compatible_printers`, `source`, `rel_path`

Returns **503** while the catalog is initialising.

404.description: Profile UUID not found in catalog

503.description: Catalog not yet ready — retry after /api/health reports catalog_loaded: true

post.summary: Start a slice job (UUID-based profile resolution)
post.description:
Upload a `.3mf` or `.stl` and specify print settings by profile UUID. The API resolves each profile's full inheritance chain, merges machine + process + filament into a `project_settings.config`, embeds it into the 3MF, then launches OrcaSlicer in the background and returns a `job_id` immediately.

**Machine identification (mutually exclusive):**
- `machine_uuid` — preferred; stable across catalog rebuilds.
- `manufacturer` + `model` + `nozzle` — tuple lookup; useful when the UUID is unknown.

**Key parameters:**
- `filament_uuids` — JSON array string, e.g. `'["uuid1"]'`; one entry per extruder slot in slot order. Single-extruder printers need exactly one entry.
- `plate` — 1-based plate number to slice. Use `1` for single-plate models.
- `export_3mf` — optional output filename. When set, OrcaSlicer embeds the GCode inside a `.3mf` instead of writing a standalone `.gcode` file. The download endpoint returns the `.3mf` in this case.
- `geometry_only_retry` — when `true` (default), if the first slice attempt fails OrcaSlicer retries with `Metadata/model_settings.config` stripped from the 3MF. This resolves failures caused by stale extruder-slot assignments baked into models exported from OrcaSlicer.
- `extra_config` — optional JSON object merged into the resolved `project_settings.config` after profile resolution. Use to override individual settings, e.g. `{"curr_bed_type": "Cool Plate"}` or `{"layer_height": "0.12"}`.

**STL files** are automatically converted to a minimal 3MF before slicing.

**Concurrency:** at most `MAX_CONCURRENT_JOBS` (default 4) jobs may be slicing simultaneously. When the limit is reached the request returns **503** — retry later.

**Job lifecycle after creation:**
```
pending → slicing → completed  (download available)
                  ↘ failed     (check error field in status response)
```
Poll `GET /api/slice/status/{job_id}` every 2–5 s, then download via `GET /api/slice/download/{job_id}` when complete.

400.description: Bad filename (null bytes, semicolons, or missing Content-Disposition filename)

422.description: Validation failure: plate < 1, filament_uuids is not a valid JSON array, machine/process/filament UUID not found in catalog, profile not compatible with the selected machine, or extra_config is not a JSON object

503.description: Profile catalog not yet ready (retry after catalog_loaded: true), or too many concurrent slice jobs (retry when a slot opens)

post.description:
Accepts a `.3mf` that already contains `Metadata/project_settings.config` (e.g., exported from OrcaSlicer desktop or produced by `POST /api/slice/start` with `export_3mf` set). No catalog lookup or profile merging is performed — OrcaSlicer uses the embedded settings verbatim.

Use this endpoint when you already have a fully-configured 3MF and want to avoid the overhead of profile resolution. Typical callers: Themis queue engine dispatching pre-packed project 3MFs, or debugging with a known-good 3MF from the desktop app.

**Geometry-only retry** works the same as in `POST /api/slice/start`: when `geometry_only_retry=true` (default) and the first slice fails, the API retries with `Metadata/model_settings.config` stripped from the 3MF.

**`export_3mf`:** when set, OrcaSlicer embeds the sliced GCode into a `.3mf` and the download endpoint returns that file instead of a standalone `.gcode`.

The response and job lifecycle are identical to `POST /api/slice/start`.

400.description: Bad filename (null bytes, semicolons, or missing Content-Disposition filename)

422.description: File is not a .3mf, or plate < 1

503.description: Too many concurrent slice jobs — retry when a slot opens (check active_jobs in GET /api/health)

post.description:
Runs OrcaSlicer with `--arrange 0 --export-3mf` to render a plate thumbnail PNG without disturbing the model's geometry or generating GCode. Returns the PNG bytes directly with `Content-Type: image/png`.

**Blocking:** waits up to 120 seconds for OrcaSlicer to finish. No job tracking — this is fire-and-forget from the caller's perspective.

The endpoint accepts only `.3mf` files. The 3MF must embed a valid plate (the requested `plate` number must exist in the model). OrcaSlicer writes the PNG to `Metadata/plate_{N}.png` inside the output 3MF; the endpoint extracts and returns that PNG, cleaning up all temporary files afterwards.

All error conditions (OrcaSlicer failure, timeout, missing PNG output) return **422** with a JSON detail object `{"error": "..."}` describing the cause.

422.description: OrcaSlicer returned non-zero, no PNG found in output, plate < 1, bad filename, or non-.3mf file uploaded. Also returned on timeout (the OrcaSlicer process is killed).

get.description:
Returns the current status of a slice job. Poll every 2–5 seconds until `status` is `completed` or `failed`.

**Response fields:**
- `job_id` — echoed back for correlation
- `status` — one of `pending`, `slicing`, `completed`, `failed`
- `output_format` — `gcode` (default) or `gcode_3mf` (when `export_3mf` was set)
- `sliced_file` — basename of the output file when `completed`, otherwise `null`
- `error` — human-readable error message when `failed`, otherwise `null`

**Job lifecycle:** `pending` → `slicing` → `completed` or `failed`

Jobs are kept for `JOB_TTL_SECONDS` (default 3600) or until downloaded. A **404** on a previously-valid `job_id` means the job has already been evicted — either downloaded or timed out.

404.description: Job not found — never existed, already downloaded, or evicted after TTL

get.description:
Returns a Server-Sent Events stream of OrcaSlicer stdout/stderr lines, emitted in real time as the slicer runs.

**Event format:** `data: <log line>\r\n\r\n`

**Terminal events (stream ends after these):**
- `data: __COMPLETED__` — job finished successfully
- `data: __FAILED__: <reason>` — job failed; `<reason>` is a short label

**Usage notes:**
- The stream replays all buffered log lines immediately on connect, so connecting after the job has already completed still delivers the full log history.
- Most callers can skip this and poll `GET /api/slice/status` instead — logs are primarily useful for debugging slice failures.
- If the job is evicted before you connect, the endpoint returns **404**.

404.description: Job not found — never existed, already downloaded, or evicted after TTL

get.description:
Returns the sliced output as `application/octet-stream`. The file extension indicates the format:
- `.gcode` — standalone GCode file (default)
- `.3mf` — GCode embedded inside a 3MF (when `export_3mf` was set during job creation)

**⚠️ One-time download:** downloading evicts the job immediately — the in-memory record and all temporary files on disk are deleted. There is no second download. Only call this once you have confirmed `status == 'completed'`.

Returns **400** if the job exists but is not yet complete (wrong status). Returns **404** if the job does not exist (never created, already downloaded, or evicted).

400.description: Job exists but is not yet complete — check status first

404.description: Job not found (never existed, already downloaded, or evicted after TTL), or job is complete but the output file is missing from disk

post.summary: Pack N STLs onto as many plates as needed (synchronous)
post.description:
Distributes STL geometry across build plates via OrcaSlicer `--arrange 1 --orient 1`. Returns the resulting multi-plate `.3mf` directly as `application/octet-stream`. **Blocks for up to 120 seconds.**

## Bed / settings source (mutually exclusive — supply exactly one)

**1. Template file** (`template` field)
Upload a `.3mf` whose `Metadata/project_settings.config` carries the printer and bed configuration. OrcaSlicer reads bed dimensions and settings from that config. Use this when you already have a known-good 3MF template from a previous session.

**2. Profile UUIDs** (`machine_uuid` + `process_uuid` + `filament_uuids`)
The container resolves profiles from its catalog, builds the settings internally, and constructs a minimal bed-only template (only `printable_area` and `printable_height` are embedded — no full machine config that could override the bed size). Templates for a given UUID combination are cached in memory, so repeated calls with the same profiles are fast. This mode requires `catalog_loaded: true`.

**3. Explicit bed dimensions** (`bed_x` + `bed_y` + `bed_z`, all in mm)
Laminus constructs a minimal bed template from the supplied dimensions. `bed_z` is the printable height (not the physical Z travel). No profile catalog or template file needed — useful when you want geometry-only packing without print settings.

## STL inputs

- `files` — one or more `.stl` files (max 50 per request)
- Filenames must not contain null bytes, newlines, or semicolons
- Duplicate filenames in the same batch are deduplicated with a numeric suffix

## Notes

- OrcaSlicer auto-orients objects (`--orient 1`) to minimise support material. Pass orient=false to skip (not yet exposed as a parameter — file a request).
- The packed `.3mf` preserves `Metadata/project_settings.config` from the template but discards any geometry or model_settings from it.
- All temporary files are cleaned up after the response is sent.

400.description: Bad filename in STL or template upload, OrcaSlicer arrange process failed (non-zero exit or missing output file), or failed to build the combined 3MF

408.description: Pack operation timed out after 120 seconds

422.description: No STL files provided, more than 50 STLs, non-.stl file in files list, non-.3mf template, bed dimensions ≤ 0, invalid filament_uuids JSON, UUID not found in catalog, or no settings source provided

500.description: Unexpected system error — check server logs

503.description: Profile catalog not yet ready (UUID mode only)

post.summary: Auto-arrange and orient objects in a 3MF (synchronous)
post.description:
Runs OrcaSlicer's plate-packing (`--arrange 1`) and auto-orientation (`--orient 1`) on an existing `.3mf` and returns the rearranged `.3mf` directly as `application/octet-stream`.

**Blocking:** waits up to `ARRANGE_TIMEOUT_SECONDS` (default 120 s) for OrcaSlicer to finish. No job tracking — call completes synchronously.

**When to use this vs `POST /api/pack`:**
- Use `POST /api/arrange` when you already have a `.3mf` with the correct geometry and want to repack it (e.g., after hand-editing or re-importing).
- Use `POST /api/pack` when starting from STL files that need to be combined into a 3MF first.

**Settings preservation:** the input 3MF's `Metadata/project_settings.config` is passed to OrcaSlicer via `--datadir`, which means OrcaSlicer may load its own machine profiles from `/config`. The `Metadata/model_settings.config` (extruder/slot assignments) from the input 3MF is preserved in the output.

All temporary files are cleaned up after the response is sent, including the stable output copy used to serve the download.

400.description: Bad filename (null bytes, semicolons, or missing Content-Disposition filename), non-.3mf file uploaded, OrcaSlicer returned non-zero exit code, or output file missing after OrcaSlicer claims success

408.description: OrcaSlicer arrange timed out — reduce model complexity or increase ARRANGE_TIMEOUT_SECONDS

500.description: Unexpected system error — check server logs

post.description:
Upload a flat OrcaSlicer preset JSON into the user config volume at `/config/user/default/{type}/`. A catalog rebuild is triggered in the background automatically — wait ~5 s and call `GET /api/profiles` to confirm the profile appears before using it in a slice job.

**Requirements for the JSON file:**
- Must have a `.json` extension
- Must be a *fully-flattened* preset with no `inherits` chain. If the profile relies on a system base profile, flatten it first using `flatten_profiles.py` inside the container: `docker exec laminus python /workspace/app/flatten_profiles.py --profile 'Elegoo Centauri Carbon 0.4 nozzle'`
- `type` must be exactly `machine`, `process`, or `filament`

**Profile persistence:** the uploaded file is written to a Docker volume (`/config/user/`). It survives container restarts but is not baked into the image. Re-upload after replacing the container with a clean image.

**UUID stability:** user profiles receive a UUID derived from `(user, rel_path)` so the UUID changes if the file is renamed or moved.

400.description: Invalid `type` (must be machine, process, or filament), bad filename (null bytes, semicolons, path traversal), or file does not have a .json extension

get.description:
Returns service readiness information. **Always call this first** — profile and slice endpoints return **503** while `catalog_loading` is `true`.

**Response fields:**
- `status` — always `"healthy"` when the process is running
- `orcaslicer_installed` — whether the OrcaSlicer binary exists at `/usr/local/bin/orcaslicer`
- `orcaslicer_version` — version string from `orcaslicer --version`, or `null` if the binary is not installed or timed out
- `config_mounted` — whether `/config` exists (volume mount check)
- `system_profiles_available` — whether the OrcaSlicer AppImage's profiles directory is present (required for catalog build)
- `catalog_loaded` — `true` when the profile catalog is fully built and ready; `false` during initial startup or rebuild
- `catalog_building` — `true` while a catalog rebuild is in progress
- `catalog_profile_count` — `{machine: N, process: N, filament: N}` when loaded, otherwise `null`
- `active_jobs` — number of jobs currently in `slicing` status

bed_x.title: Bed X
bed_x.description: Bed X dimension in mm (alternative to template / UUIDs)

bed_z.title: Bed Z
bed_z.description: Bed Z printable height in mm

extra_config.title: Extra Config
extra_config.description: JSON object merged into project settings after resolution

machine_uuid.title: Machine Uuid
machine_uuid.description: Stable UUID of the machine (printer) profile. Obtain from `GET /api/profiles` or `GET /api/profiles/{uuid}`. Machine UUIDs are derived from the (manufacturer, model, nozzle) tuple and are stable across catalog rebuilds.

process_uuid.title: Process Uuid
process_uuid.description: Stable UUID of the process (print quality) profile, e.g. `0.20mm Standard`. Must be compatible with the supplied machine — check `compatible_printers` in the profile detail.

filament_uuids.title: Filament Uuids
filament_uuids.description: List of filament profile UUIDs, one per extruder slot in slot order (slot 1 first). Must be compatible with the machine. Single-extruder printers need exactly one entry.

tags.name: health
tags.description: Service readiness and version information. Always check `/api/health` first — profile and slice endpoints return **503** while `catalog_loading` is `true`.

tags.name: profiles
tags.description: Machine, process, and filament preset catalog derived from the OrcaSlicer AppImage's bundled profiles plus any user-uploaded profiles in `/config/user/`. Profiles are identified by stable UUIDs derived from their (manufacturer, model, nozzle) tuple (machine) or (source, rel_path) (process/filament), so UUIDs remain constant across catalog rebuilds as long as the profile files don't move.

tags.name: slice
tags.description: Asynchronous slice job lifecycle: **start → poll → download**. Jobs are kept in memory and evicted after download or after `JOB_TTL_SECONDS` (default 3600). A maximum of `MAX_CONCURRENT_JOBS` (default 4) jobs may slice simultaneously; additional requests receive **503** until a slot opens.

tags.name: arrange
tags.description: Synchronous plate arrangement and STL packing. No job tracking — responses block until OrcaSlicer finishes (up to `ARRANGE_TIMEOUT_SECONDS`, default 120). Use `POST /api/pack` to combine multiple STLs into a single multi-plate `.3mf`, and `POST /api/arrange` to re-pack an existing `.3mf`.
